	{"bbox_2d": [164, 544, 900, 583], "text_content": "This pin allows the incoming VBUS power from the USB Type-C receptacle to be enabled when a source is connected according to different operating conditions stated in the table below. 	{"bbox_2d": [399, 604, 696, 615], "text_content": "Table 6. VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 624, 279, 652], "text_content": "NVM parameter\nPOWER_ONLY\n_ABOVE_5V"},

	{"bbox_2d": [211, 690, 233, 700], "text_content": "0b"},
	{"bbox_2d": [201, 702, 243, 712], "text_content": "(default)"},
	{"bbox_2d": [330, 680, 447, 724], "text_content": "Enables VBUS power path\nwhen source attached\nwhatever VBUS voltage (5 V or\nany PDO voltage)"},

	{"bbox_2d": [830, 690, 894, 740], "text_content": "Valid for all\nPOWER_OK pin\nconfigurations 1, 2\nand 3"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 757, 233, 767], "text_content": "1b"},

	{"bbox_2d": [164, 812, 912, 837], "text_content": "When POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V bit is set to logic level high, the VBUS_EN_SNK pin is asserted only when a\nPDO2 or PDO3 explicit contract is established with the source (see Section 3.3 Auto-run mode)."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 840, 916, 865], "text_content": "This feature is suited for sink devices requiring high power charging profile above 5 V to be fully operational (see\nSection 6.1.2 Powering a system under high charging profile only)."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 868, 893, 893], "text_content": "VBUS_EN_SNK pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to\nenable the VBUS power path."},
